question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: resolveRegisteredNodeIdBounds(List<NumberRangeParameter> registeredNodeIdRanges)] Can an unprivileged external user pass two range parameters both using `gte` operator to `resolveRegisteredNodeIdBounds()`, causing `Math.max(lowerBound, range.getInclusiveValue())` to be called twice and silently select the higher lower bound, effectively hiding all registered nodes with IDs below that value and presenting a truncated view of the historical node registration sequence?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: resolveRegisteredNodeIdBounds(List<NumberRangeParameter> registeredNodeIdRanges)] Can an unprivileged external user pass two range parameters both using `lte` operator to `resolveRegisteredNodeIdBounds()`, causing `Math.min(upperBound, range.getInclusiveValue())` to be called twice and silently select the lower upper bound, effectively hiding all registered nodes with IDs above that value and presenting a truncated view of the historical node registration sequence?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getLatestNetworkStake()] Can an unprivileged external user repeatedly call `getLatestNetworkStake()` via `GET /api/v1/network/stake` to observe changes in `NetworkStake` fields over time, reconstructing the historical sequence of staking period changes and inferring when specific staking events occurred, effectively reorganizing the visible staking history without any authentication?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getLatestNetworkStake()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `getLatestNetworkStake()` endpoint returning only the single latest record (via `findLatest()` using `max(consensus_timestamp)`) to infer that historical stake data is inaccessible through this path, and then combine it with supply history queries to reconstruct a timeline of staking changes that misrepresents the actual historical ordering of consensus events?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getSupply(Bound timestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user pass a `timestamp` with `gte` operator set to a value that, after `getAdjustedLowerRangeValue()` increments it by 1, falls exactly on a balance snapshot boundary, causing `accountBalanceRepository.getSupplyHistory()` to include or exclude that snapshot depending on off-by-one behavior and returning a supply figure that misrepresents the historical state at that nanosecond?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getSupply(Bound timestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user pass a `timestamp` with `lt` operator set to a value that, after `adjustUpperBound()` decrements it by 1, falls exactly on a balance snapshot boundary, causing `accountBalanceRepository.getSupplyHistory()` to exclude that snapshot and return a supply figure from the previous snapshot, effectively shifting the apparent historical supply state by one record?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getSupply(Bound timestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user pass two timestamp parameters with `gte` and `lte` operators that produce an effective range spanning exactly one nanosecond, causing `getFirstDayOfMonth(maxTimestamp, -1)` to compute an `optimalLowerBound` that is larger than the user-supplied `minTimestamp`, silently widening the query window and returning supply data from outside the requested historical range?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getSupply(Bound timestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `getFirstDayOfMonth()` method's use of `ZoneOffset.UTC` and `plusMonths(-1)` to craft a `maxTimestamp` in a month with 31 days where the previous month has 28 days (February), causing the computed `optimalLowerBound` to land on a different day than expected and silently truncating the historical supply query window?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getSupply(Bound timestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user pass a `timestamp` with `lte` set to a value where `adjustUpperBound()` returns `Long.MAX_VALUE - 1` (due to `lt` operator decrement), causing `getFirstDayOfMonth(Long.MAX_VALUE - 1, -1)` to overflow or produce an unexpected date, resulting in an `optimalLowerBound` that reorganizes which historical supply records are visible?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getSupply(Bound timestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user pass a `timestamp` with `gt` operator set to 0 so that `getAdjustedLowerRangeValue()` returns 1, and then `Math.max(1, optimalLowerBound)` selects `optimalLowerBound` (the first day of the month before `maxTimestamp`), causing the supply history to start from a computed date rather than the genesis of the chain and hiding the earliest historical supply records?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getNetworkNodes(NetworkNodeRequest request)] Can an unprivileged external user pass `file.id=eq:101` to `getNetworkNodes()` to query the proxy address book (file 101) instead of the consensus address book (file 102), causing `findNetworkNodes()` to join against a different `address_book` record and return a historically different set of nodes, allowing the attacker to compare the two address books and reconstruct the history of network topology changes?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getNetworkNodes(NetworkNodeRequest request)] Can an unprivileged external user pass `node.id=gte:0` and `node.id=lte:Long.MAX_VALUE` to `getNetworkNodes()` so that `lowerBound=0` and `upperBound=Long.MAX_VALUE`, causing `findNetworkNodes()` to return all nodes in the latest address book ordered by `node_id ASC`, allowing full reconstruction of the current consensus committee without any authentication?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getNetworkNodes(NetworkNodeRequest request)] Can an unprivileged external user pass multiple `node.id=eq:X` parameters to populate `nodeIds` with multiple values, causing `findNetworkNodes()` to use `abe.node_id = any(:nodeIds)` and return only those specific nodes, allowing targeted enumeration of specific historical node entries and their associated `start_consensus_timestamp` values to reconstruct when those nodes joined the network?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getNetworkNodes(NetworkNodeRequest request)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `latest_address_book` CTE in `findNetworkNodes()` which selects `order by start_consensus_timestamp desc limit 1` to always return only the most recent address book, and then use `file.id` parameter variations to observe whether different file IDs return different `start_consensus_timestamp` values, reconstructing the history of address book updates?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getNetworkNodes(NetworkNodeRequest request)] Can an unprivileged external user pass `node.id=eq:X` for a node ID that exists in the `node` table but not in the current `address_book_entry`, causing the `join latest_address_book` to exclude it and return an empty result, while a range query would include it, creating an inconsistency in the apparent historical node membership that could be exploited to misrepresent network topology history?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getNetworkNodes(NetworkNodeRequest request)] Can an unprivileged external user observe that `findNetworkNodes()` returns `endConsensusTimestamp` from `address_book.end_consensus_timestamp`, and by querying with different `file.id` values, reconstruct the complete timeline of address book validity periods, effectively reorganizing the visible history of which nodes were active during which consensus periods?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getNetworkNodes(NetworkNodeRequest request)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `latest_node_stake` CTE in `findNetworkNodes()` which uses `max(consensus_timestamp)` to always return the latest stake data, causing the returned `stakingPeriod` and `stake` values to reflect the current state rather than the historical state at the time of the queried address book, creating a misleading view of historical staking that misrepresents the transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getNetworkNodes(NetworkNodeRequest request)] Can an unprivileged external user pass `limit=1` with `order=ASC` and then `order=DESC` in separate requests to observe the first and last node IDs in the address book, and by binary-searching with `node.id=gt:X` parameters, reconstruct the complete ordered sequence of historical node IDs without triggering any rate limiting or authentication checks?
